headline: Chief Operating Officer at QMS Media
summary:
An extensive leadership career in the OOH sector, my commitment to the growth and success of the industry has seen me play a pivotal role in the creation and management of some its most well known brands - Eye Corp, oOh! Media and QMS Media.

In my current role as Chief Operating Officer at QMS , I have the privilege to oversee all functions of the business outside of sales, and help guide our incredible talent to work together to achieve a high performance culture.

I am exceptionally proud of all we have achieved at QMS since its inception 10 years ago. From a start up to a publicly listed company on the ASX, to successfully delisting and selling to private equity in a little over five years. It has been an incredible story so far, that still has so many exciting chapters to be written and I am grateful to be able to be a part of it.

experiences.title: Chief Marketing Officer
experiences.description: Responsible for the brand development and management of QMS in Australia and its associated entities in Indonesia and New Zealand.  Overseeing all trade marketing activities, events and promotions, as well as the implementation of our business and corporate communications strategy.

experiences.title: Director
experiences.description: Verve is a marketing consultancy business specialising in developing and managing specific marketing projects for businesses to help build brand equity and quality client engagement.

experiences.title: Group Director - Marketing
experiences.description:
Responsible for creating and developing the oOh! brand following the merge of two outdoor media businesses - Network Outdoor & Media Puzzle. Consistently delivered engaging go-to-market strategies and effective communications that helped shape the business as a leader in the industry.

Key Outcomes:
- Successful, well recognised and highly engaged brand
- Business achieved 12% growth during the GFC in 2009 while rest of the outdoor industry declined by 12%
- EBITDA grew from $4m to $23m during my time overseeing marketing & communications at oOh!

Key Responsibilities:
- Creation of oOh! brand
- Responsible for all go-to-market strategies and communications for both the brand and products
- Trade marketing strategies and creative direction
- Oversaw the development of social media strategies
- Oversaw the development of online and multimedia strategies 
- Key liaison with brand and creative agencies
- Budget development, reconciliation and reporting
- Effective people management and mentoring skills across a team of 8
- Developed and delivered numerous trade promotions
- Event management
- Overall business strategy development as part of senior management team
- Responsible for brand refresh following acquisition of EYE Corp in 2012

experiences.title: State Sales Manager - VIC
experiences.description:
Led the Melbourne sales team to effectively market and sell all outdoor solutions including billboards, airports and shopping centres.

Key responsibilities:
- Managed the Melbourne sales team
- Developed, monitored and reconciled the team and individual sales budgets
- Set targets and KPI's
- Developed sales collateral and promotions for all products
- Created product integration strategies and concepts

experiences.title: Group Sales Manager - Eye Drive
experiences.description:
Led the Melbourne Drive sales team to effectively market and sell large format billboards to advertisers and advertising agencies.

Key responsibilities:
- Managed a team of four sale representatives in Melbourne
- Developed, monitored and reconciled the team and individual sales budgets
- Set targets and KPI's
- Developed sales collateral and promotions for the Drive product

experiences.title: Key Accounts Manager - Eye Shop
experiences.description: Responsible for the development and launching of 'mall media' or retail shopping centre advertising in Australia.  Included development of key sales collateral and materials, promotions and selling to key clients.

experiences.title: Strategic Marketing Analyst
experiences.description: Responsible for creating and developing the EYE brand following the rebrand of outdoor media business - NLD Outdoor. Developed initial launch and go-to-market strategies, prior to the acquisition of EYE by Network Ten which led to all marketing functions being relocated to Sydney.

recommendations:
Brendon Cook

Sara was employed by oOh! Media as Marketing Director for more than seven years after the business purchased Media Puzzle (MP). Prior to acquiring MP, I had seen from afar the excellent brand and marketing communications work Sara had delivered in the creation of the Eye Corp brand. Whilst at oOh!, Sara was responsible for creating what has been considered in the media industry as one of the preeminent brands and positioning. The oOh! brand was developed to re-position Network Outdoor and Media Puzzle as a combined new business with scale, and to be seen as an entrepreneurial, can-do business in the out-of-home industry. Sara excelled in the development and ongoing brand building of oOh! that saw the business become the only media company with over $50 million in sales to grow by 12% during the GFC (note the outdoor sector was down 12%). During her time at oOh!, Sara was also an integral part of the senior management team and active in business development and strategies that enabled oOh! to grow to $130 million in revenue, with EBITDA growth from $4m to $23m. Through strategic go-to-market trade activities and communications that delivered positive engagements with our clients, oOh!’s brand equity has also grown in line with the business. Overseeing a team of nine people, Sara has experience in nurturing and mentoring people to achieve their best and to work at the highest level, with the enthusiasm and passion required to be successful. I have nothing but the highest praise for Sara and her brand and marketing achievements, and her overall business skill set.

Stephanie Gasperini

I had the pleasure of working closely with Sara on an intensive large-scale project that spanned many months. Without a doubt, Sara is a dedicated marketing professional who sets the bar high for herself and those around her. Sara is intuitive, collaborative and open to the ideas of others. She is an excellent communicator, with an eye for detail, who builds long term relationships based on trust.
